requirements.text: All user-facing error messages MUST be human-readable (no stack traces exposed)
API responses MUST include machine-readable error codes alongside messages
README MUST describe the project purpose, setup steps, and usage examples
CLI commands MUST provide --help output describing all flags
Public API functions MUST include usage examples in docstrings
Configuration options MUST have documented defaults and valid ranges
Error messages MUST suggest corrective actions when possible
Long-running operations MUST provide progress indication or logging
Destructive operations MUST require confirmation or use --force flags
Exit codes MUST be non-zero for error conditions in CLI tools
Input MUST be validated with clear error messages indicating what is wrong
Dangerous defaults MUST NOT exist; fail-safe defaults are required
Ambiguous string formats (dates, numbers) MUST be rejected with format hints
Required fields MUST be enforced at the type level, not just at runtime
Output format MUST be consistent across all commands (JSON or table, not mixed)
Log messages MUST use consistent severity levels and structured format
Terminal output MUST respect NO_COLOR and TERM environment variables
UI components MUST include ARIA labels or semantic HTML elements
Colour MUST NOT be the sole means of conveying information
Interactive elements MUST be keyboard-navigable
Images MUST include alt text descriptions
